contract.goal: Close out Phase 3's remaining loose end discovered when /ev-loop-interactive attempted the Phase 4 whiteboard step. The Phase 3 audit applied 'Substrate-internal primitives pin to disable-model-invocation: true' to guild-spawn, guild-validate, and guild-whiteboard — but ev-loop composes these via the Skill tool, and the flag blocks model-driven Skill composition (the same trap #102 just fixed for ev-loop-*). Remove the flag from all three guild-* skills; keep them user-invocable: false (they remain internal primitives, not top-level commands). Update PLAN.md to document the followup-2 section, amend the audit-rubric Decision to correct the source-of-the-trap rule, and pin a new Decision for the guild-* canonical shape.
acceptance_criteria: `.claude/skills/guild-spawn/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/guild-validate/SKILL.md`, and `.claude/skills/guild-whiteboard/SKILL.md` no longer contain `disable-model-invocation: true`. All three retain `user-invocable: false`.
PLAN.md has a new `### Phase 3 followup 2: guild-* frontmatter correction` subsection between the existing Phase 3 followup and Phase 4 sections.
PLAN.md's audit-rubric Decision (the two-axis frontmatter rubric) is amended to correct the 'pin internal substrate primitives to disable-model-invocation: true' rule that was the source of the trap.
PLAN.md has a new Decision pinning the canonical guild-* shape (user-invocable: false + disable-model-invocation: false) against the original audit intent.
PLAN.md Dependencies section has a new bullet: 'Phase 3 followup 2 precedes Phase 4' parallel to the existing followup-precedes-Phase-4 line.
rules_applied: L-002 halt-and-fork was applied at dispatch: discovered the substrate gap during Phase 4 whiteboard-step setup, halted Phase 4, surfaced multiple named options, picked the consistent option (separate followup PR mirroring #102's shape) rather than silently bundling into Phase 4.

L-013 grounding (the bug claim was confirmed via the actual Skill-tool error 'cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation' returned when /ev-loop-interactive tried to compose /guild-whiteboard, not inferred from frontmatter reading).
Substrate-no-freestyle (feedback memory): PLAN edit goes through `bin/draft revise`; manifest is not touched (no phase changes); frontmatter-only edits to three SKILL.md files.
Frontmatter-only edits to SKILL.md files; no body prose touched.

L-004-shape session-boundary note: smoke verification of /ev-loop-interactive → /guild-whiteboard composition was observed in-session (the available-skills list updated after edits, mirroring the in-session pickup we saw for ev-loop-* in #102). Full composition smoke deferred to next session for harness-reload confirmation.
disqualifiers: Edit /griot-load's frontmatter (the Phase 4 design pins it as `disable-model-invocation: true` + `user-invocable: true`; no Skill composition path exists for it).
Add a new manifest phase row (loom lacks `phase add`; the followup is documented in PLAN only, same pattern as #102).
Bundle Phase 4 work into this PR (followup-2 is a single conceptual change: extending #102's pattern to guild-*).

actions: Discovered the gap mid-session: /ev-run substrate-cli dispatched to /ev-loop-interactive for Phase 4, which attempted /guild-whiteboard via Skill, which failed with 'cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'. Same trap as #102, different family.

Halted Phase 4: switched back to main, rolled back the phase 4 in-progress flag (`bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 4 --status=not-started`), reverted the orphan substrate writes (events.jsonl phase-started event + manifest branch-name reference), and deleted the local substrate-cli/phase-4 branch.
Cut substrate-cli/phase-3-followup-2 from main (at ea59446 post-#102 squash).
Edited all three guild-* SKILL.md files via Edit tool: removed `disable-model-invocation: true` from frontmatter; kept `user-invocable: false`.

Composed revised PLAN.md in /tmp/loom-revision-substrate-cli.md: added `### Phase 3 followup 2: guild-* frontmatter correction` subsection (one deliverable + branch + verification + 'One PR.'), amended the audit-rubric Decision to correct the 'Substrate-internal primitives pin to disable-model-invocation: true' rule, added a new Decision pinning the canonical guild-* shape, added Dependencies bullet placing the followup 2 before Phase 4. Preserved all prior `## Revision log` entries verbatim per the substrate finding from the 2026-05-16 entry.

Ran `bin/draft revise substrate-cli --revision-file=/tmp/loom-revision-substrate-cli.md --rationale=...`. PLAN.md replaced; new revision-log entry appended by the CLI; CLI also committed automatically per substrate convention.

Verified in-session: all three guild-* skills now appear in the harness's available-skills list (Skill-registry pickup of frontmatter changes is in-session, matching what #102 observed for ev-loop-*).
Ran `npm run lint`: clean. Two pre-existing symlink warnings for .claude/skills/vercel-react-best-practices (unrelated).
corrections: correction: Phase 3's audit-rubric rule 'Substrate-internal primitives pin to disable-model-invocation: true' is wrong. The flag's semantics block ALL model-driven Skill invocation, not just ambient routing. Any skill composed via the Skill tool by another skill — even an internal substrate primitive — must be `disable-model-invocation: false`. The correct shape for an internal primitive composed via Skill is `user-invocable: false` + `disable-model-invocation: false`. This is the second occurrence of the same pattern bug (#102 was ev-loop-*; this is guild-*); future audits should smoke-test the actual composition path, not just classify by intent.

scope: Three SKILL.md frontmatter edits (guild-spawn, guild-validate, guild-whiteboard).
One PLAN.md revision via bin/draft revise (new followup-2 section + Decision amendment + new Decision pin + Dependencies bullet).
changes_since_previous: First checkin on substrate-cli/phase-3-followup-2 branch. Baseline: origin/main at ea59446 (post-#102 squash merge).
notes_for_pr: Phase 3 closeout (second followup), not a new phase. Manifest Phase 3 row stays `completed`; PLAN.md captures followup-2 as a subsection under Phase 3, mirroring how #102 documented the first followup.

Bug surfaced when /ev-loop-interactive (dispatched by /ev-run for Phase 4) tried to compose /guild-whiteboard for the always-on whiteboard pre-step. The Skill tool refused with `cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation` — identical error shape to the original Phase 4-dispatch trap that #102 fixed for ev-loop-*.

Recurring pattern observed: the Phase 3 audit set `disable-model-invocation: true` on internal primitives composed by other skills. The original intent ('not ambient routing') is correct; the flag mapping is too strict. Two corrections have now landed: #102 for ev-loop-* (composed by /ev-run) and this PR for guild-* (composed by /ev-loop-*). Future skill audits should smoke-test the actual Skill-composition path as part of the audit's verification step, not just classify on intent.

Substrate observation reinforced: Claude Code's skill registry picks up SKILL.md frontmatter changes mid-session — all three guild-* skills appeared in the available-skills list after the edits without a restart. This is the second observation of the in-session pickup behavior (the first was during #102). Worth promoting to a learning via /griot-capture if it stays consistent.

Verification: a true end-to-end smoke of /ev-loop-interactive → /guild-whiteboard composition is most cleanly done in a fresh session after this PR merges (the harness saw the original disable-model-invocation flag at session start). Lint clean; PLAN.md documents the work; on-disk frontmatter is correct.
